arguments.abstract: Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Get started with Tuist in your Xcode project or create a generated project.

arguments.abstract: The path to the directory from where to start. (env: TUIST_INIT_PATH)
Base64-encoded prompt answers (env: TUIST_INIT_PATH)
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Utilities to debug the hashing logic used by features like binary caching or selective testing.

arguments.abstract: Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Returns the hashes that will be used to persist binaries of the graph in its current state to the cache.

arguments.abstract: The path to the directory that contains the project whose targets will be cached. (env: TUIST_HASH_CACHE_PATH)
The project configuration the cache binaries will be bound to. (env: TUIST_HASH_CACHE_CONFIGURATION)
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Returns the hashes that will be used to persist targets' test results to select tests in future test runs.

arguments.abstract: The path to the directory that contains the project whose tests will run selectively. (env: TUIST_HASH_CACHE_PATH)
When running tests selectively through 'tuist xcodebuild test', the additional 'xcodebuild' arguments that you'd pass, some of which are hashed.
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Builds a project

arguments.abstract: The scheme to be built. By default it builds all the buildable schemes of the project in the current directory. (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_SCHEME)
Force the generation of the project before building. (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_GENERATE)
Force the generation of the project before building. (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_GENERATE)
When passed, it cleans the project before building it (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_CLEAN)
When passed, it cleans the project before building it (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_CLEAN)
The path to the directory that contains the project to be built. (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_PATH)
Build on a specific device. (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_DEVICE)
Build for a specific platform. (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_PLATFORM)
Build with a specific version of the OS. (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_OS)
When passed, append arch=x86_64 to the 'destination' to run simulator in a Rosetta mode. (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_ROSETTA)
When passed, append arch=x86_64 to the 'destination' to run simulator in a Rosetta mode. (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_ROSETTA)
The configuration to be used when building the scheme. (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_CONFIGURATION)
The directory where build products will be copied to when the project is built. (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH)
[Deprecated] Overrides the folder that should be used for derived data when building the project. (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_DERIVED_DATA_PATH)
When passed, it generates the project and skips building. This is useful for debugging purposes. (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_GENERATE_ONLY)
When passed, it generates the project and skips building. This is useful for debugging purposes. (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_GENERATE_ONLY)

Arguments that will be passed through to xcodebuild. Use -- followed by xcodebuild arguments. Example: tuist build -- -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15' -sdk iphonesimulator (env: TUIST_BUILD_OPTIONS_PASSTHROUGH_XCODE_BUILD_ARGUMENTS)
Ignore binary cache and use sources only. (env: TUIST_BUILD_BINARY_CACHE)
Ignore binary cache and use sources only. (env: TUIST_BUILD_BINARY_CACHE)
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Warms the local and remote cache.

arguments.abstract: The path to the directory that contains the project whose targets will be cached. (env: TUIST_CACHE_PATH)
Configuration to use for binary caching. (env: TUIST_CACHE_CONFIGURATION)

A list of targets to cache. Those and their dependant targets will be cached. If no target is specified, all the project targets (excluding the external ones) and their dependencies will be cached. (env: TUIST_CACHE_TARGETS)
If passed, the command doesn't cache the targets passed in the `--targets` argument, but only their dependencies (env: TUIST_CACHE_EXTERNAL_ONLY)
If passed, the command doesn't cache the targets passed in the `--targets` argument, but only their dependencies (env: TUIST_CACHE_EXTERNAL_ONLY)
When passed, it generates the project and skips warming the cache. This is useful for debugging purposes. (env: TUIST_CACHE_GENERATE_ONLY)
When passed, it generates the project and skips warming the cache. This is useful for debugging purposes. (env: TUIST_CACHE_GENERATE_ONLY)
When passed, the hashes of the cacheable frameworks in the given project are printed. (env: TUIST_CACHE_PRINT_HASHES)
When passed, the hashes of the cacheable frameworks in the given project are printed. (env: TUIST_CACHE_PRINT_HASHES)
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Clean all the artifacts stored locally

arguments.abstract: The cache and artifact categories to be cleaned. If no category is specified, everything is cleaned. (env: TUIST_CLEAN_CLEAN_CATEGORIES)
arguments.defaultValue: plugins, generatedAutomationProjects, projectDescriptionHelpers, manifests, editProjects, runs, binaries, selectiveTests, dependencies

arguments.abstract: Clean the remote cache (env: TUIST_CLEAN_REMOTE)
Clean the remote cache (env: TUIST_CLEAN_REMOTE)
The path to the directory that contains the project that should be cleaned. (env: TUIST_CLEAN_PATH)
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Outputs the manifest as a JSON

arguments.abstract: The path to the folder where the manifest is (env: TUIST_DUMP_PATH)
The manifest to be dumped (env: TUIST_DUMP_MANIFEST)
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Generates a temporary project to edit the project in the current directory

arguments.abstract: The path to the directory whose project will be edited (env: TUIST_EDIT_PATH)
It creates the project in the current directory or the one indicated by -p and doesn't block the process (env: TUIST_EDIT_PERMANENT)
It creates the project in the current directory or the one indicated by -p and doesn't block the process (env: TUIST_EDIT_PERMANENT)
It only includes the manifest in the current directory. (env: TUIST_EDIT_ONLY_CURRENT_DIRECTORY)
It only includes the manifest in the current directory. (env: TUIST_EDIT_ONLY_CURRENT_DIRECTORY)
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Generates an Xcode workspace to start working on the project.

arguments.abstract: The path to the directory or a subdirectory of the project. (env: TUIST_GENERATE_PATH)

Targets to focus on, specified by name or tag query (e.g. 'tag:feature'). Other targets will be linked as binaries if possible. If no target is specified, all the project targets will be generated (except external ones, such as Swift packages).
Don't open the project after generating it. (env: TUIST_GENERATE_OPEN)
Don't open the project after generating it. (env: TUIST_GENERATE_OPEN)
Ignore binary cache and use sources only. (env: TUIST_GENERATE_BINARY_CACHE)
Ignore binary cache and use sources only. (env: TUIST_GENERATE_BINARY_CACHE)
Configuration to generate for.
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Generates a graph from the workspace or project in the current directory

arguments.abstract: Skip Test targets during graph rendering. (env: TUIST_GRAPH_SKIP_TEST_TARGETS)
Skip Test targets during graph rendering. (env: TUIST_GRAPH_SKIP_TEST_TARGETS)
Skip external dependencies. (env: TUIST_GRAPH_SKIP_EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCIES)
Skip external dependencies. (env: TUIST_GRAPH_SKIP_EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCIES)
A platform to filter. Only targets for this platform will be showed in the graph. Available platforms: ios, macos, tvos, watchos (env: TUIST_GRAPH_PLATFORM)
Available formats: dot, json, png, svg (env: TUIST_GRAPH_FORMAT)
Don't open the file after generating it. (env: TUIST_GRAPH_OPEN)
Don't open the file after generating it. (env: TUIST_GRAPH_OPEN)
Available formats: dot, neato, twopi, circo, fdp, sfdp, patchwork (env: TUIST_GRAPH_LAYOUT_ALGORITHM)
A list of targets to filter. Those and their dependent targets will be showed in the graph. (env: TUIST_GRAPH_TARGETS)
The path to the directory that contains the project whose targets will be cached. (env: TUIST_GRAPH_PATH)
The path where the graph will be generated. (env: TUIST_GRAPH_OUTPUT_PATH)
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Installs any remote content (e.g. dependencies) necessary to interact with the project.

arguments.abstract: The path to the directory or a subdirectory of the project. (env: TUIST_INSTALL_PATH)
Instead of simple install, update external content when available. (env: TUIST_INSTALL_UPDATE)
Instead of simple install, update external content when available. (env: TUIST_INSTALL_UPDATE)
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: A set of utilities to assist in the migration of Xcode projects to Tuist.

arguments.abstract: Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: It extracts the build settings from a project or a target into an xcconfig file.

arguments.abstract: The path to the Xcode project (env: TUIST_MIGRATION_SETTINGS_TO_XCCONFIG_XCODEPROJ_PATH)
The path to the .xcconfig file where build settings will be extracted. (env: TUIST_MIGRATION_SETTINGS_TO_XCCONFIG_XCCONFIG_PATH)
The name of the target whose build settings will be extracted. When not passed, it extracts the build settings of the project. (env: TUIST_MIGRATION_SETTINGS_TO_XCCONFIG_TARGET)
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: It checks if the build settings of a project or target are empty. Otherwise it exits unsuccessfully.

arguments.abstract: The path to the Xcode project (env: TUIST_MIGRATION_CHECK_EMPTY_SETTINGS_XCODEPROJ_PATH)
The name of the target whose build settings will be checked. When not passed, it checks the build settings of the project. (env: TUIST_MIGRATION_CHECK_EMPTY_SETTINGS_TARGET)
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: It lists the targets of a project sorted by number of dependencies.

arguments.abstract: The path to the Xcode project (env: TUIST_MIGRATION_LIST_TARGETS_XCODEPROJ_PATH)
Show help information.

Build both source and test targets. (env: TUIST_PLUGIN_BUILD_BUILD_TESTS)

Print the binary output path. (env: TUIST_PLUGIN_BUILD_SHOW_BIN_PATH)

Build the specified targets. (env: TUIST_PLUGIN_BUILD_TARGETS)
Build the specified products. (env: TUIST_PLUGIN_BUILD_PRODUCTS)

arguments.abstract: Choose configuration (default: debug). (env: TUIST_PLUGIN_OPTIONS_CONFIGURATION)

Skip building the plugin. (env: TUIST_PLUGIN_RUN_SKIP_BUILD)

The arguments to pass to the plugin task. (env: TUIST_PLUGIN_RUN_ARGUMENTS)

Build both source and test targets. (env: TUIST_PLUGIN_TEST_BUILD_TESTS)

subcommands.abstract: Runs a scheme or target in the project

arguments.abstract: Runnable project scheme, a preview URL, or app name with a specifier such as App@latest or App@feature-branch. (env: TUIST_RUN_SCHEME)
Force the generation of the project before running. (env: TUIST_RUN_GENERATE)
Force the generation of the project before running. (env: TUIST_RUN_GENERATE)
When passed, it cleans the project before running. (env: TUIST_RUN_CLEAN)
When passed, it cleans the project before running. (env: TUIST_RUN_CLEAN)
The path to the directory that contains the project with the target or scheme to be run.
The configuration to be used when building the scheme.
The simulator or physical device name to run the target, scheme, or preview on.
The OS version of the simulator. (env: TUIST_RUN_OS)
When passed, append arch=x86_64 to the 'destination' to run simulator in a Rosetta mode.
Arguments to pass to the application during execution. All arguments after the scheme name are forwarded to the app. Example: tuist run MyApp --verbose --config debug (env: TUIST_RUN_ARGUMENTS)
Show help information.

subcommands.discussion:
Given a runnable scheme or target the run command builds & runs it.
All arguments after the scheme or target are forwarded to the application.

subcommands.abstract: Generates new project based on a template

arguments.abstract: Name of template you want to use (env: TUIST_SCAFFOLD_TEMPLATE)
The output in JSON format (env: TUIST_SCAFFOLD_JSON)
The output in JSON format (env: TUIST_SCAFFOLD_JSON)
The path to the folder where the template will be generated (Default: Current directory) (env: TUIST_SCAFFOLD_PATH)
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Lists available scaffold templates

arguments.abstract: The output in JSON format (env: TUIST_SCAFFOLD_LIST_JSON)
The output in JSON format (env: TUIST_SCAFFOLD_LIST_JSON)
The path where you want to list templates from (env: TUIST_SCAFFOLD_LIST_PATH)

subcommands.abstract: Tests a project

arguments.abstract: The scheme to be tested. By default it tests all the testable targets of the project in the current directory. (env: TUIST_TEST_SCHEME)
When passed, it cleans the project before testing it. (env: TUIST_TEST_CLEAN)
When passed, it cleans the project before testing it. (env: TUIST_TEST_CLEAN)
When passed, the result necessary for test selection is not persisted to the server. (env: TUIST_TEST_NO_UPLOAD)
When passed, the result necessary for test selection is not persisted to the server. (env: TUIST_TEST_NO_UPLOAD)
The path to the directory that contains the project to be tested. (env: TUIST_TEST_PATH)
Test on a specific device. (env: TUIST_TEST_DEVICE)
Test on a specific platform. (env: TUIST_TEST_PLATFORM)
Test with a specific version of the OS. (env: TUIST_TEST_OS)
When passed, append arch=x86_64 to the 'destination' to run simulator in a Rosetta mode. (env: TUIST_TEST_ROSETTA)
When passed, append arch=x86_64 to the 'destination' to run simulator in a Rosetta mode. (env: TUIST_TEST_ROSETTA)
The configuration to be used when testing the scheme. (env: TUIST_TEST_CONFIGURATION)
When passed, it skips testing UI Tests targets. (env: TUIST_TEST_SKIP_UITESTS)
When passed, it skips testing UI Tests targets. (env: TUIST_TEST_SKIP_UITESTS)
Path where test result bundle will be saved. (env: TUIST_TEST_RESULT_BUNDLE_PATH)
[Deprecated] Overrides the folder that should be used for derived data when testing a project. (env: TUIST_TEST_DERIVED_DATA_PATH)
[Deprecated] Tests will retry <number> of times until success. Example: if 1 is specified, the test will be retried at most once, hence it will run up to 2 times. (env: TUIST_TEST_RETRY_COUNT)
The test plan to run. (env: TUIST_TEST_TEST_PLAN)
The list of test identifiers you want to test. Expected format is TestTarget[/TestClass[/TestMethod]]. It is applied before --skip-testing (env: TUIST_TEST_TEST_TARGETS)
The list of test identifiers you want to skip testing. Expected format is TestTarget[/TestClass[/TestMethod]]. (env: TUIST_TEST_SKIP_TEST_TARGETS)
The list of configurations you want to test. It is applied before --skip-configuration (env: TUIST_TEST_CONFIGURATIONS)
The list of configurations you want to skip testing. (env: TUIST_TEST_SKIP_CONFIGURATIONS)
Ignore binary cache and use sources only. (env: TUIST_TEST_BINARY_CACHE)
Ignore binary cache and use sources only. (env: TUIST_TEST_BINARY_CACHE)
When --no-selective-testing is passed, tuist runs all tests without using selective testing. (env: TUIST_TEST_SELECTIVE_TESTING)
When --no-selective-testing is passed, tuist runs all tests without using selective testing. (env: TUIST_TEST_SELECTIVE_TESTING)
When passed, it generates the project and skips testing. This is useful for debugging purposes. (env: TUIST_TEST_GENERATE_ONLY)
When passed, it generates the project and skips testing. This is useful for debugging purposes. (env: TUIST_TEST_GENERATE_ONLY)
When passed, run the tests without building. (env: TUIST_TEST_WITHOUT_BUILDING)
When passed, run the tests without building. (env: TUIST_TEST_WITHOUT_BUILDING)
When passed, build the tests, but don't run them (env: TUIST_TEST_BUILD_ONLY)
When passed, build the tests, but don't run them (env: TUIST_TEST_BUILD_ONLY)
Arguments that will be passed through to xcodebuild. Use -- followed by xcodebuild arguments. Example: tuist test -- -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15' -parallel-testing-enabled YES
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: tuist xcodebuild extends the xcodebuild CLI with server capabilities such as selective testing or analytics.

arguments.abstract: Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: tuist xcodebuild test extends the xcodebuild CLI test action with insights and selective testing capabilities.

arguments.abstract: Arguments that will be passed through to the xcodebuild CLI. All arguments are forwarded to xcodebuild. Example: tuist xcodebuild test -scheme MyAppTests -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15' -parallel-testing-enabled YES
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: tuist xcodebuild test-without-building extends the xcodebuild CLI test action with insights and selective testing capabilities.

arguments.abstract: Arguments that will be passed through to the xcodebuild CLI. All arguments are forwarded to xcodebuild. Example: tuist xcodebuild test-without-building -scheme MyAppTests -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15' -testConfiguration Debug
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: tuist xcodebuild build extends the xcodebuild CLI build action with insights.

arguments.abstract: Arguments that will be passed through to the xcodebuild CLI. All arguments are forwarded to xcodebuild. Example: tuist xcodebuild build -scheme MyApp -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15'
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: tuist xcodebuild build-for-testing extends the xcodebuild CLI build-for-testing action with insights.

arguments.abstract: Arguments that will be passed through to the xcodebuild CLI. All arguments are forwarded to xcodebuild. Example: tuist xcodebuild build-for-testing -scheme MyAppTests -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15'
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: tuist xcodebuild archive extends the xcodebuild CLI archive action with insights.

arguments.abstract: Arguments that will be passed through to the xcodebuild CLI. All arguments are forwarded to xcodebuild. Example: tuist xcodebuild archive -scheme MyApp -archivePath ./build/MyApp.xcarchive
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Generate a link to share your app.

arguments.abstract: The path to the directory that contains a Tuist or Xcode project with a buildable scheme that can output runnable artifacts.
The app name to be looked up in the built products directory or the paths to the app bundles or an .ipa archive. (env: TUIST_SHARE_APP)
The configuration of the app to share. Ignored when the app paths are passed directly. (env: TUIST_SHARE_CONFIGURATION)
The platforms (iOS, tvOS, visionOS, watchOS or macOS) to share the app for. Ignored when the app paths are passed directly. (env: TUIST_SHARE_PLATFORM)
The derived data path to find the apps in. When absent, the system-configured one. (env: TUIST_SHARE_DERIVED_DATA_PATH)
The output in JSON format. (env: TUIST_SHARE_JSON)
The output in JSON format. (env: TUIST_SHARE_JSON)
Show help information.

subcommands.abstract: Shows information about a bundle.

arguments.abstract: The ID of the bundle to show. (env: TUIST_BUNDLE_SHOW_ID)
The full handle of the project. Must be in the format of account-handle/project-handle. If not provided, it will be read from the project's Tuist.swift. (env: TUIST_BUNDLE_SHOW_FULL_HANDLE)
The path to the directory or a subdirectory of the project. (env: TUIST_BUNDLE_SHOW_PATH)
The output in JSON format. (env: TUIST_BUNDLE_SHOW_JSON)
The output in JSON format. (env: TUIST_BUNDLE_SHOW_JSON)
Show help information.

Organization ID for your SSO provider. For Google, this is your Google domain (for example, if your email is tuist@tuist.dev, the domain would be tuist.dev). For Okta, it's the organization domain (such as my-org.okta.com) (env: TUIST_ORGANIZATION_UPDATE_SSO_ORGANIZATION_ID)
